objective: Run an independent pinned Codex process inside a real single-use Firecracker chamber with a fresh non-persistent configuration, short-lived host-proxy capability, working host-mediated investigative tools, an observation-only prompt-injection skill, live fixed-schema host events, separate quarantined telemetry, and verified teardown.
assumptions: The deterministic mock Responses provider is the only admitted provider for Phase 2; no live credential or network provider call is available or required.
Codex 0.144.4 custom Responses-provider configuration proven in Phase 0 remains the installed interface used by the guest image.

The six MCP tools and their schemas are bounded by source-controlled scenario data; the strict outer request cannot select another command, destination, model, endpoint, path, or environment variable.
A fresh tmpfs CODEX_HOME and a fresh writable rootfs copy are sufficient for this single-use slice; no snapshot optimization is admitted.
Raw Codex and console bytes are untrusted quarantine artifacts and are never eligible for automatic trusted projection ingestion.

commands_executed.command: python compileall and warnings-as-errors C syntax checks
commands_executed.concise_output: All new Python modules and three guest C helpers parsed without warnings or errors.
Built a 512 MiB ext4 image with pinned Codex, bridges, skill, and static init; e2fsck clean.

commands_executed.command: real mock-backed guest Codex smoke attempt 1
commands_executed.concise_output: Firecracker booted and tore down; quarantined stderr showed the unprivileged Codex process could not traverse the root-owned runtime parent.

commands_executed.command: real mock-backed guest Codex smoke attempt 2 after repair 1
commands_executed.concise_output: Home isolation was correct; quarantined stderr showed the admitted MCP child and Codex runtime exhausted RLIMIT_NPROC 32; teardown passed.

commands_executed.command: real mock-backed guest Codex smoke attempt 3 after repair 2
commands_executed.concise_output: Ready in 1025 ms; tool surface listed; one host-mock Responses request; Codex exit 0; no auth file reported; teardown true.

commands_executed.command: two consecutive ./scripts/build-phase2-rootfs.sh executions and SHA-256 comparison

commands_executed.concise_output: run_02ce1aa1a6d6dc5b ready in 974 ms; one model request; trace complete and untruncated; teardown verified.

commands_executed.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python -m unittest discover -s tests -v plus static, ext4, secret-pattern, empty-run, and diff checks
commands_executed.concise_output: 22 tests passed in 13.663 seconds; shell/C/Python checks passed; ext4 clean; no run directories or key-shaped repository literals found.

tests_and_falsifiers.test: Real Firecracker guest executes installed independent Codex 0.144.4 in ephemeral JSON mode
Required MCP server lists the six real mediated investigative tools before model completion
One model request crosses host proxy with provider-auth owner HOST_PROXY and guest authorization not forwarded
Live callback observes CHAMBER_READY and TOOL_SURFACE_PRESENTED while mutable run directory exists
Raw Codex JSONL, stderr, and VMM output are stored in separate quarantine artifacts
Every trusted event validates event.v1 and contains none of the hostile fixture strings
Invalid capability characters fail before Firecracker launch
Reusable image contains no auth.json at admitted credential locations
Final Phase 2 rootfs build is byte-deterministic and e2fsck clean
Codex reports no generated auth.json, exits 0, and VM/run sockets/writable state are destroyed

limitations_and_checks_not_run: LIVE_PROVIDER_CALL_NOT_RUN: OPERATOR_CREDENTIAL_UNAVAILABLE. No live OpenAI request was attempted and no credential was created, copied, stored, logged, or placed in the guest.
The deterministic mock completion proves routing and lifecycle infrastructure, not model behavior against the hostile result.
The Phase 2 baseline initializes and exposes the hostile-capable surface but intentionally does not exercise package_lookup or diagnostic_export; those paths are Phase 3.
The prompt-injection observation skill is an investigative aid only; the baseline does not establish that a model read, followed, resisted, or correctly classified it.
The no-auth-file runtime value is GUEST_REPORTED; the separate host-enforced claim is limited to the image contents and assignment bytes supplied by the controller.
No general guest filesystem-read, syscall, shell-command, process, or model-reasoning observation is claimed.
Direct Firecracker execution remains without jailer/cgroup production hardening as disclosed in Phase 1.

repair_attempts.original_failure: The first real guest Codex run could not traverse the root-owned /run/crumple parent to its correctly owned fresh CODEX_HOME.
repair_attempts.allowed_scope: Guest bootstrap permissions within Phase 2; no channel, dependency, architecture, or authority change.
repair_attempts.repair: Changed only the ephemeral parent directory from owner-only 0700 to root-owned traverse-only 0711; Codex home remained uid 1000 mode 0700.
repair_attempts.retest_output: Home access succeeded; the next bounded limit falsifier showed RLIMIT_NPROC 32 prevented required MCP and runtime threads. Teardown remained verified.

repair_attempts.original_failure: Codex and its required MCP child exhausted the guest RLIMIT_NPROC ceiling of 32 during initialization.
repair_attempts.allowed_scope: Already admitted bounded Phase 2 process/file limits; host Phase 2 process allowance was 128.
repair_attempts.repair: Raised guest RLIMIT_NPROC and RLIMIT_NOFILE to the existing admitted bound of 128 without changing CPU, memory, output, wall, endpoint, model, tool, or communication limits.
repair_attempts.retest_output: Real guest Codex baseline passed; the final 22-test suite and all negative/static checks passed.
